article_title: Three-Quarters of Enterprise AI Customer Service Deployments Are Rolled Back, New Study Finds
summary: Submission approved: all statistics verified against primary sources. Allowlist warnings for sinch.com (primary source) and capacityglobal.com are false positives — both are legitimate and verified by editor.

content_preview.title: Three-Quarters of Enterprise AI Customer Service Deployments Are Rolled Back, New Study Finds
content_preview.summary: A Sinch survey of 2,527 AI decision-makers finds 74% have shut down live AI customer communications agents, with rollback rates rising to 81% among the most governance-mature organizations.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Nearly three-quarters of enterprises that have deployed AI customer communications agents have been forced to shut them down or roll them back — and the problem is worse, not better, for organizations with the most sophisticated safety controls. That is the central finding of *The AI Production Paradox*, a global study published on May 13 by Swedish communications-as-a-service firm [Sinch](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/sinch-research-reveals-74-of-enterprises-have-rolled-...

recommendations: Consider adding sinch.com and capacityglobal.com to config/source_allowlist.txt.

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong News article (802 words, upper News range). Covers a vendor-sponsored survey with appropriate transparency about the conflict of interest in the 'What We Don't Know' section — this is commendable journalistic practice.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 5 source snapshots. source-0.html.gz (prnewswire.com): Confirmed 74% rollback rate, 2,527 respondents, 10 countries, 6 industries, Daniel Morris CPO title, guardrail tax quote, 81% mature guardrails rollback rate, 84%/98%/75%/63%/55% statistics. source-1.html.gz (sinch.com/blog): Primary research source — confirmed all statistics including 0.52 correlation, 31%/22%/16% failure causes, 35%/34% downstream effects, 42%/37%/32% infrastructure gaps, Anton Efimenko SVP title, his full quote. source-2.html.gz (sinch.com/ai-production-paradox): Research chapter confirmed 74%/81% rollback rates, 31% PII leading cause, 22% hallucination, 16% auditability. source-3.html.gz (capacityglobal.com): Confirmed Daniel Morris quote ('The most advanced organisations aren't failing less; they're seeing failures sooner') verbatim. source-4.html.gz (theregister.com): Confirmed 74%/81% rates, 75%/63% spending priorities. All quotes verified verbatim or substantively confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All statistics verified across multiple sources. The Daniel Morris quotes are confirmed: the shorter one ('The most advanced organisations aren't failing less...') is in capacityglobal.com; the longer quote ('If governance was the fix...') is in sinch.com blog — both correctly attributed in the article. Anton Efimenko quote confirmed in sinch.com.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and critical. The article appropriately flags the vendor bias in the 'What We Don't Know' section. No editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate coverage of this specific Sinch report found in archive.
recommendations: Consider adding sinch.com and capacityglobal.com to config/source_allowlist.txt.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Well-constructed News piece that covers a vendor survey with appropriate skepticism while accurately reporting all verified statistics. APPROVE.
